skills.name: brain-access
skills.description: Use when the user wants to manage API keys, tenant memberships, or access control. Activate when the conversation involves creating/revoking API keys, adding/removing users from tenants, changing user roles, or managing access credentials.

skills.name: brain-files
skills.description: Use when the user wants to upload, download, list, or delete files, or attach/detach files to note parts. Activate when the conversation involves file management, file attachments, or MinIO-backed storage in the Brain platform.

skills.name: brain-note-groups
skills.description: Use when the user wants to create, manage, or organize notes into groups. Activate when the conversation involves note grouping, collections, projects grouping notes, adding/removing notes from groups, or listing groups within a tenant.

skills.name: brain-notes
skills.description: Use when the user wants to create, read, update, delete, archive, or share notes through the Brain MCP server. Activate when the conversation involves note content, note visibility, note sharing/permissions, archiving, or listing notes within a tenant.

skills.name: brain-search
skills.description: Use when the user wants to search across notes and atomic parts. Activate when the conversation involves finding notes by content, semantic search, or full-text search in the Brain platform.

skills.name: brain-tenants
skills.description: Use when the user wants to create, manage, or switch tenants, or generate tenant-scoped API tokens. Activate when the conversation involves tenant CRUD, multi-tenancy, or generating scoped tokens for a specific tenant.

skills.name: deploy
skills.description: Deploy a tagged release to the k3s cluster via Helm. Use when the user wants to deploy, roll out, or ship a version. Handles release-pull mode (download binary from GitHub Release) and verifies the deployment is healthy before reporting success.

skills.name: mcp-builder
skills.description: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).

skills.name: skill-creator
skills.description: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.

skills.name: skill-router
skills.description: Catalog, fetch, and install skills from the PJ-SBN-593844 GitHub organization on demand. Maintains a registry of known skill repos and manages them as git submodules under .claude/skills/.

skills.name: tag-create
skills.description: Compute the next strict semver tag (v*.*.*) from conventional-commit history and create an annotated local tag with a grouped changelog body. Use when the user wants to cut a release locally without pushing. Does not push.

skills.name: tag-push
skills.description: Push an existing local v*.*.* tag to origin. Use when the user explicitly asks to publish a release tag. Pushing a v* tag triggers the CI release build, so this skill requires explicit confirmation and never force-pushes.

skills.name: tag-release
skills.description: Cut a release end-to-end — compute the next v*.*.* tag from commits, create the annotated tag locally, then push to origin. Use when the user wants a single command that publishes a release. Includes an explicit confirmation gate before pushing because pushing a v* tag triggers the CI release build.

skills.name: working-with-rules
skills.description: Enforces all project rules from memory-bank/rules/. This skill MUST be followed at all times during any task — it loads every rule file and requires strict compliance.
